clips.note: Living Constitutionalist and Originalist clash over public use versus public purpose.
Federalism Hawk flips to affirm, citing state police power over local planning.
Living Constitutionalist flips to reverse, persuaded by dignity and non-blighted sanctuary arguments.
Living Constitutionalist flips back to affirm, convinced by Pragmatist's warning on urban paralysis.
Initial threshold and merits debate: standing, precedent, federalism, liberty, and minimalist off-ramps.
Pragmatist and Civil Libertarian fiercely debate holdout vetoes versus individual dignity and liberty.
Round two opening: Pragmatist warns of text traps, Originalist and Precedent Maximalist respond.
Pragmatist defends assembly math; Civil Libertarian, Federalism Hawk, and Process Formalist push back.
Final clash: Pragmatist demands workable blight standards, Civil Libertarian defends home as sanctuary.
Precedent Maximalist and Process Formalist argue over state finality versus federal Article III jurisdiction.
